prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: "And so it came to pass in the third quarter of this strange and bewildering year that the scrolls of ‘ARR’ did swell by a full 27%, yet the elders of the ‘CAC’ lamented a churn most grievous, as if a plague of locusts had descended upon the land of ‘MRR’; the mystical ‘burn rate’ waxed and waned like the moon, confounding even the wisest of soothsayers; and though the company’s ‘runway’ stretched long as the Nile, the scribes of ‘GAAP’ and ‘non-GAAP’ quarreled endlessly over the true measure of treasure; verily, I, Brother Aldric, have recorded these marvels, though they defy all logic known to God and man."
